notes: sha256 values for exe/iso are copied from each entry's metadata.json; cue SHAs are computed at manifest-gen time.
metadata.json and build.log were retained long enough to build this manifest, then dropped with the payloads.

regeneration_command uses the repo's existing build-ps1.sh + make-cd-image.sh at the originating commit. Hash match across time is not guaranteed (Docker image drift, mkpsxiso timestamps, etc); see sample_rebuild_proof below.

archive_destination for exe/iso/cue/build-log: DELETED (not archived). Per user decision on 2026-04-22, payloads were removed outright rather than archived externally. If a specific historical binary is ever needed, rebuild from the commit SHA using the regeneration_command - non-deterministic per sample_rebuild_proof.
The CSV preserves the pre-deletion retention decisions as a historical record; fields like retention_decision=archive_out_of_repo were ultimately executed as delete rather than archive.

prerequisites_beyond_git: jc_resources/RESOURCE.MAP and jc_resources/RESOURCE.001 (original game asset bundle; gitignored)
jc_resources/extracted/, transcoded/, packs/ (regenerable from the original bundle; gitignored)
interpretation: Reproducibility PROVEN in the sense that the historical commit builds and produces a structurally valid .bin/.cue from the retained metadata + gitignored asset bundle.

Byte-identical reproducibility NOT proven: the .exe diverges (104448 vs 139264 bytes) because the build path fell back to 'manual PS1 link' at this commit — CMake target 'jcreborn' wasn't resolvable against the current build system. The .bin matches within 0.1% size (37KB delta) which is consistent with mkpsxiso volume-timestamp embedding, per plan note.

Retention decision for the archived binaries therefore stands on their SHA256 + commit provenance, not on the ability to reconstruct identical bits. If a future session needs a specific historical binary byte-for-byte, it must come from the archive, not from rebuild.
